ground_truth.title: Tuition and Fees | Tuition and Estimated Costs
ground_truth.main_content:
Tuition is set annually by the Florida Legislature, with additional local and university fees set by the Florida State University Board of Trustees and the Florida Board of Governors. You can find regulatory references for tuition and fee assessment through Title XLVIII, Chapter 1009, Part II of Florida's Statutes, BOG Regulation 7.001 and 7.003 of the State University System of Florida Board of Governors, and Chapter FSU-2.02421 Tuition and Fee Assessment and Refunds of Florida State University Regulations. The following rate tables apply to the 2025-2026 academic year and are subject to change based on legislative and internal policy changes. Some courses are assessed additional costs for online fees, labs, materials and supplies, and general cost recovery. For a full list of these courses and costs, download the Additional Cost File.

Traditional Academic Programs tuition rates include: Main Campus Undergraduate at $215.55 per credit hour (in-state) and $771.66 (out-of-state); Main Campus Graduate at $479.32 per credit hour (in-state) and $1,173.86 (out-of-state); Panama City Campus Undergraduate at $180.49 (in-state) and $736.60 (out-of-state); Panama City Campus Graduate at $444.26 (in-state) and $1,138.80 (out-of-state); Sarasota Campus Undergraduate at $182.30 (in-state) and $738.41 (out-of-state); Sarasota Campus Graduate at $446.07 (in-state) and $1,140.61 (out-of-state); Distance Learning Undergraduate at $180.49 (in-state) and $736.60 (out-of-state); Distance Learning Graduate at $444.26 (in-state) and $1,138.80 (out-of-state); College of Law - Main at $688.11 (in-state) and $1,421.89 (out-of-state); College of Law - Online at $653.05 (in-state) and $1,386.83 (out-of-state); Republic of Panama at $300.00 (both in-state and out-of-state for undergraduate) and $400.00 (both for graduate).

Special Medical Programs include: Nurse Anesthesia - Panama City at $9,500.44 per semester (all cohorts, flat rate); Physician Assistant - Main Campus at $31,463.04 per year (in-state) and $43,013.04 (out-of-state).

College of Medicine rates are: Year 1 at $8,536.86 per semester (in-state) and $21,205.63 (out-of-state); Year 2 at $12,805.30 per semester (in-state) and $31,808.45 (out-of-state); Years 3-4 at $8,536.87 per semester (in-state) and $21,205.63 (out-of-state); All Years at less than 6 Hours at $479.32 per credit hour (in-state) and $1,173.86 (out-of-state).

Academic Program Specific Fees include: Media Production Equipment Fee at $113.00 per semester for all Media Production Majors; Medical Equipment Fee at $345.00 per semester for all Medical Students; Music Equipment Fee at $350.00 per semester for all Music Majors; Film Equipment Fee at $350.00 per semester for all College of Motion Picture Arts Majors; Nursing Equipment Fee ranging from $25.00 to $310.00 per semester depending on the specific nursing program; Art Equipment Fee at $125.00 per semester for all Art Majors; Art History Equipment Fee at $80.00 per semester for all Art History Majors; Dance Equipment Fee at $125.00 per semester for all Undergraduate Dance Majors; Interior Design Equipment Fee at $135.00 per semester for all Interior Design Majors; Theatre Equipment Fee at $115.00 per semester for all Theatre Majors.
with: Tuition is set annually by the Florida Legislature, with additional local and university fees set by the Florida State University Board of Trustees and the Florida Board of Governors.
The following rate tables apply to the 2025-2026 academic year and are subject to change based on legislative and internal policy changes.
Main Campus Undergraduate tuition is $215.55 per credit hour for in-state students and $771.66 for out-of-state students.
College of Medicine Year 1 tuition is $8,536.86 per semester for in-state students and $21,205.63 for out-of-state students.
Academic Program Specific Fees include Media Production Equipment Fee at $113.00 per semester and Medical Equipment Fee at $345.00 per semester.
